bypassStrategy: Do NOT navigate via Puppeteer (page.goto/page.click) — FamilySearch blocks headless navigation
Launch Chrome via CLI with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and log in manually
Manually navigate to first branch collection image
Use spawnSync+osascript to inject page numbers via React input setter (bypasses shell quoting issues)
Extract table data via read-only page.evaluate
ensureImageIndexTab() must be called before each extraction to guard against Information tab being active
protocol.deduplication: ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (unique on source_url = record_ark per depositor)

safetyValves.tabGuard: ensureImageIndexTab() clicks tab--0 before every extractRecords() call

columnMapping.cells[0]: More button (has button=true, sticky overlay, skip)
columnMapping.cells[2]: Name — the primary depositor name (can be EMPTY when person is referenced only by relationship)
columnMapping.cells[3+]: Variable: Mother's Name, Mother's Sex, Spouse's Name, Event Place, Account Number — cells omitted when empty

tableStructure.headerWarning: Header shows 'Mother's Sex' twice at positions [4] and [5] — likely a display label bug. Second is Spouse's Name.
tableStructure.extractionStrategy: Content-based classification — scan cells[2:] for first name-like value as full_name, then classify rest by pattern
tableStructure.knownQuirk: React virtualization causes column bleeding: when Name (cells[2]) is empty, Mother's Name bleeds into cells[2]. Child rows (George, Carrie Fraser, etc.) may show as mother's name in DB. This is expected and acceptable — ARKs are unique so ON CONFLICT prevents true duplication.

criticalColumns.locations: text[] NOT jsonb — pass JS array directly to neon, do NOT JSON.stringify or cast ::jsonb
criticalColumns.source_url: Stores individual record ARK (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:XXXX-XXXX) for future enrichment

formerEnslaverAccess.detail: Former enslaver, age, occupation live in the 1:1: individual record pages which require FamilySearch login. Programmatic access via Platform API (/platform/records/search) returns 404. Cross-domain fetches to sg30p0.familysearch.org are blocked by CORS. Information tab only shows catalog metadata, not record-level fields.
formerEnslaverAccess.futureEnrichment: When FamilySearch API OAuth credentials are obtained, use source_url (record_ark) to fetch /platform/records/personas/{id} for each depositor

procedures.dryRun: Add --dry-run flag: no DB writes, prints extracted JSON to stdout
procedures.promotion: Only run after full ingestion; via promote-trackable-to-canonical.js
procedures.garbagedRecordsHistory: 65,126 garbage records deleted 2026-04-10 — caused by old blob-based extraction storing full page innerHTML as full_name with all structured columns null

bugsFixedSession_2026_04_11: AppleScript bridge used shell single-quotes which broke names with apostrophes → fixed with spawnSync (args passed directly, no shell)
extractRecords used cells[2] hardcoded as name → broke when Name column empty; fixed with scan-first-name-like-value approach over cells[2:]
locations column passed as JSON.stringify(arr)::jsonb → cast error; fixed by passing JS array directly (neon serializes natively to text[])
Information tab pollution: clicking Information tab left it active, scraper then extracted catalog metadata as person records → fixed with ensureImageIndexTab() called before every page extract
table tr querySelectorAll matched header row and non-data rows → fixed with tr[data-testid] selector (only data rows have ARK testIds)

bugsFixedSession_2026_04_15.summary: Full rewrite of Freedmen's Bank indexed scraper after audit found 9,334 corrupted rows and oscillating pagination. Charleston re-scraped cleanly: 15,854 rows, 0.03% initial garbage, 0 errors.

architecture.navigation: puppeteer-native (osascript removed). Initial page.goto() to target image, then "Next Image" button clicks with 4s fixed wait for React viewer to render.
architecture.onBranchViewer: detected via wc= waypoint parameter, NOT ark ID. Each image in a roll has its own ark; only wc is stable across a roll.
architecture.extraction: children[2] is always the Name cell (sometimes <th scope="row">, sometimes <td> after React hydration). Other cells classified by content (digit-only=account, geo-commas=place, Male/Female=sex-column-skip, prose=source-note-reject, name-like=family_member).
architecture.noteFilter: looksLikeNote() rejects strings >40 chars, >5 words, or containing sentence words (with,came,his,husband,wife,etc) so source-text ledger notes don't leak into family_members.
architecture.reactTableCaveats: FS Image Index table is React-virtualized with variable column positions per row (adult rows and child rows have different cell layouts despite same 13-child count). Positional column mapping via header row is UNRELIABLE. Use children[2] for name + content classification for everything else.

bugsFixedSession_2026_04_15.branchesMapFormat: { total: <totalImages>, url: <full viewer URL with wc+ark+i=N> }. Verify each branch by opening its URL in Chrome and confirming Image Index tab shows populated rows before adding. Prior multi-branch map was unverified and broken (wrong wc waypoints).
bugsFixedSession_2026_04_15.chromeSetup: Separate Chrome instance on --remote-debugging-port=9222 with --user-data-dir=/tmp/chrome-scraper. Must have at least one FS tab open and logged in. Tab state can break between runs — reload via puppeteer if table disappears.
bugsFixedSession_2026_04_15.purgedGarbage: 9,334 rows from initial audit + 5 residual post-run = 9,339 total garbage rows cleared.
bugsFixedSession_2026_04_15.nextBranches: Richmond, Wilmington, Raleigh — user will provide verified ark viewer URLs one at a time. Old committed BRANCHES map had indexUrl-style URLs that are not compatible with the new ark-based navigation.
